cards.back: A collection of elements of the same data type stored in contiguous memory locations
An array with elements stored in a single row, either horizontally (left-to-right) or vertically (top-to-bottom)
An array with elements stored in multiple rows and columns, allowing for rectangular-shaped data structures

cards.front: What is the main difference between a one-dimensional array and a two-dimensional array?
cards.back: The main difference is that a two-dimensional array can store rectangular-shaped data, while a one-dimensional array can only store linear data

cards.front: How do you initialize a two-dimensional array?
cards.back: A two-dimensional array is initialized using square brackets and commas, e.g., int arr[2][3] = {{1, 2, 3}, {4, 5, 6}};
